description_long: This is a bulbous, hand-crafted clay vase with a wide, rounded body that tapers gently toward a slightly flared rim. The surface is matte and unglazed, showing natural variations in color from pale beige to light terracotta, with subtle textural irregularities and small pitting typical of traditional pottery. The rim is thick and uneven, suggesting it was shaped by hand rather than machine. There are no visible handles or decorative embellishments, giving the vase a utilitarian, rustic appearance. One side of the vase features a faint, hand-written marking or catalog number, indicating it may have been part of a collection or inventory. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with vessels intended for holding water, plants, or other contents.
description: A rustic, hand-shaped clay vase with a wide, rounded body, flared rim, and matte, unglazed surface in pale terracotta tones. The vase is plain, with subtle surface texture and a faint catalog marking on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the vase's wide, rounded body and thick, uneven rim, highlighting the natural clay texture and subtle color variations.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the vase's bulbous lower half and the gentle taper toward the rim, with minimal visible markings.
description_view_2: This side displays a faint hand-written catalog number on the upper body, with the rim and body shape clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view presents the plainest side of the vase, showing the smooth, unadorned surface and the full curvature of the form.
